title: glandora prostrata subsp. lusitanica
intro: Glandora prostrata subsp. lusitanica, also known as Rock Rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family that is native to Portugal. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been found to have medicinal properties in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Glandora prostrata subsp. lusitanica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and even exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Rock Rose can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
